title: `$mem_v2` cells are not stored in `module->memories`, causing some passes to be unaware of them
body:
I suspect this may already be a known issue, but just in case it isn't I'm writing it up because it caught me out today.

Edit: I've had a bit more of a play and this may just be misunderstanding about what is meant to go on and what the behaviour was before, in which case just close the issue.

As I understand it, memories used to be stored separately to cells in `module->memories`, whereas with the recent memory work by mwk they are now instead represented with `$mem_v2` cells. There seem to be some passes that are unaware of this change and thus are incorrectly only checking `module->memories` for the existence of memories.

A lot of these passes are ones that don't work on unmapped memories so try to check if there are memories using something like `module->memories.size() > 0` or `module->has_memories()`. This means they miss `$mem_v2` cells which can lead to a `$mem_v2` cell being referenced in output without an implementation provided (e.g. write_spice/edif/blif), or in the case of write_aiger,, a warning message isn't produced.

This also means that `stat` displays 0 memories, which is potentially confusing, although this may be intended behaviour.
